name: ldap-utils
title: ldap-utils
description:
OpenLDAP utilities
This package provides utilities from the OpenLDAP (Lightweight
Directory Access Protocol) package. These utilities can access a
local or remote LDAP server and contain all the client programs
required to access LDAP servers.

arguments.description: Enable SASL Interactive mode. Always prompt. Default is to prompt only as needed.
arguments.raw: -I  Enable SASL Interactive mode. Always prompt. Default is to prompt only as needed.

arguments.description: what would be done, but don't actually perform the compare. Useful for debugging
arguments.raw: -n  what would be done, but don't actually perform the compare. Useful for debugging

arguments.description: Run in verbose mode, with many diagnostics written to standard output.
arguments.raw: -v  Run in verbose mode, with many diagnostics written to standard output.

arguments.description: Prompt for simple authentication. This is used instead of specifying the password on
arguments.raw: -W  Prompt for simple authentication. This is used instead of specifying the password on

arguments.description: Use simple authentication instead of SASL.
arguments.raw: -x  Use simple authentication instead of SASL.

commands.name: ldapmodify
commands.description: LDAP modify entry and LDAP add entry tools

commands.name: ldappasswd
commands.description: change the password of an LDAP entry

commands.name: ldapadd
commands.description: LDAP modify entry and LDAP add entry tools

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ldapcompare because ldapcompare - LDAP compare tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ldapcompare as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ldapcompare  [-V[V]]  [-d debuglevel] [-n] [-v] [-z] [-M[M]] [-x] [-D binddn] [-W] [-w passwd]` as documented upstream for ldapcompare.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ldapcompare "uid=babs,dc=example,dc=com"  sn:Jensen` as documented upstream for ldapcompare.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ldapcompare "uid=babs,dc=example,dc=com"  sn::SmVuc2Vu` as documented upstream for ldapcompare.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ldapcompare with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ldapcompare --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -I

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install openldap (2.6.10+dfsg-1). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `ldapcompare "uid=babs,dc=example,dc=com"  sn:Jensen`. Further invocations from upstream: `ldapcompare "uid=babs,dc=example,dc=com"  sn::SmVuc2Vu`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ldapcompare - LDAP compare tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: ldapcompare - LDAP compare tool.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install openldap (2.6.10+dfsg-1)
Install with: sudo apt install openldap (2.6.10+dfsg-1)
